title: Authentication — API Keys and OAuth 2.0
content:
CloudDash offers two authentication mechanisms for API access:

**1. API Keys (recommended for server-to-server)**
Generate API keys from Settings → Developer → API Keys.
Pass the key in every request header:
```
Authorization: Bearer cd_live_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```
Key types:
- **Read-only:** Can GET metrics, dashboards, alerts. Cannot modify resources.
- **Read-write:** Full API access. Rotate every 90 days.
- **Admin:** Includes user management and billing endpoints. Restrict carefully.

Key rate limits:
- Starter: 100 requests/minute
- Growth: 500 requests/minute
- Scale/Enterprise: 2,000 requests/minute (higher on request)

**2. OAuth 2.0 (recommended for user-facing integrations)**
CloudDash supports the Authorization Code flow with PKCE.
- Authorization endpoint: https://auth.clouddash.io/oauth/authorize
- Token endpoint: https://auth.clouddash.io/oauth/token
- Available scopes: metrics:read, alerts:write, billing:read, admin:write

Access tokens expire after 1 hour. Use the refresh token to obtain a new access token (refresh tokens expire after 30 days or on first use).
